name: Cash Nevada
description_short: Las Vegas check cashing and payday loan storefront serving locals since 1995. Offers short-term cash advances and check cashing at a physical location in the 89110 zip code.
description_long:
Cash Nevada is a locally operated check cashing and payday loan company that has served the Las Vegas and Henderson, Nevada area since its founding on October 26, 1995. Now approaching 30 years in business, the company operates as a brick-and-mortar storefront at 1117 N Nellis Blvd in Las Vegas — a neighborhood-focused model that predates the rise of online lending. Cash Nevada is not BBB-accredited, and the BBB's profile notes insufficient information to issue a formal rating, which is not uncommon for small, independently operated storefronts of this type.

Cash Nevada's core offerings are payday loans (also called payday advances) and check cashing — the two staple services of the short-term consumer cash industry. Payday loans are typically small-dollar, short-term advances against an upcoming paycheck, designed to bridge gaps between pay periods. Check cashing allows customers to convert paper checks into immediate cash without needing a bank account. No fee schedule or loan pricing has been publicly verified for Cash Nevada; prospective customers should contact them directly at (702) 438-3333 for current rates. The company operates Monday through Friday from 9am to 6pm and Saturday from 10am to 2pm, and is closed on Sundays. No online application portal or mobile app has been identified.

What distinguishes Cash Nevada most visibly is its customer satisfaction record: the business holds a 5.0 out of 5.0 Google rating drawn from 201 reviews — an unusually high score for any financial services provider, let alone a payday lender. This suggests a consistent, positive in-person experience over many years. Operating for nearly three decades in the same Las Vegas market also signals a degree of community trust that many short-term lenders lack. The company reportedly has a presence across both Las Vegas and the Henderson area, though the exact number of locations is unconfirmed.

Cash Nevada is a straightforward, no-frills option for Las Vegas-area residents who need fast cash in person. Its strengths are longevity, local roots, and a genuinely impressive customer review record. The main limitations are transparency-related: fee structures are not publicly posted, there is no online access for applications or account management, and the BBB profile offers no formal rating. As with all payday lenders, consumers should be aware that short-term cash advance products typically carry high effective APRs and are best used as a last resort for genuine short-term emergencies rather than recurring financial needs.
address: 1117 N Nellis Blvd, Las Vegas, NV 89110
pros: Founded October 1995 — nearly 30 years of continuous local operation in Las Vegas
Exceptional 5.0/5.0 Google rating from 201 reviews — rare for a payday lender
Saturday hours (10am–2pm) accommodate customers who cannot visit on weekdays
In-person storefront allows face-to-face service and same-day cash access
Serves both Las Vegas and Henderson area, providing geographic convenience for east valley residents
cons: No website or online application — customers must visit the physical location in person
Fee schedule and loan rates are not publicly available, making cost comparison difficult before visiting
BBB profile lists no formal rating due to insufficient information — limited third-party accountability data
Closed Sundays — no weekend emergency access on the most common day off
No mobile app or digital account management tools of any kind
best_for: Las Vegas and Henderson residents who need same-day cash and can visit in person
Unbanked or underbanked consumers who need to cash checks without a bank account
Customers who prefer face-to-face financial transactions over online services
East Las Vegas neighborhood residents near the Nellis Blvd corridor
diagnosis: Cash Nevada is best suited for unbanked or underbanked Las Vegas residents who need fast, in-person access to short-term cash advances or check cashing and are comfortable transacting at a physical storefront. The main caveat is that fee and rate information is not publicly disclosed, so consumers should ask directly about costs before proceeding — payday loan products carry high APRs by nature and should only be used for genuine short-term emergencies.
